deployment_question: How does the model transfer to unseen regulatory contexts such as cell type, tissue, assay, or variant-effect task?
definition: Hold out cell types, tissues, assays, or task contexts to evaluate regulatory sequence models under context transfer.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: sets same-context train overlap to zero while sequence overlap may remain

implementation_recipe: Choose whether the target novelty is cell type, tissue, assay, or task.
Hold out complete contexts and train on the remaining contexts.
Measure locus and sequence overlap separately from context overlap.
quality_gates: Avoid leaving the same locus in train and test when testing locus generalization.
Report performance by task type and context.
Check whether embeddings or preprocessing used held-out context labels.
failure_modes: Cross-context splits can still leak locus-specific signal.
Cell type and assay shifts can be confounded.
Zero-shot context labels may be unavailable at deployment.

citations.title: The ENCODE Imputation Challenge
citations.evidence: Evaluates epigenomic imputation models across cell types and highlights complexity under data shifts.

citations.title: DART-Eval: A Comprehensive DNA Language Model Evaluation Benchmark on Regulatory DNA
citations.evidence: Benchmarks DNA language models across regulatory DNA tasks and reports inconsistent performance.
